brief.title: aion-awl CI WIRING (finale part B): wire `aion awl check` over the tests/fixtures/*.awl checker-fixture set into .github/workflows/ci.yml with a mechanically-visible bounded_cycle expected-failures carve-out pointing at AWL1-004, AND wire the crate-wide zero-bypass grep as a PERMANENT CI gate. A TINY diff scoped to ONE file (.github/workflows/ci.yml). Depends on AWL0-REFAC-001e-a landing. Split from the AWL0-REFAC-001e finale per operator right-sizing ruling.
brief.objective: Wire two additive CI gates into .github/workflows/ci.yml at ZERO cost to any other file, touching ONLY that one file. TWO concerns. (1) awl-check job — add an additive fast-lane job (mirror the additive `codegen-drift`/`no-norn-in-platform` convention: actions/checkout@v4, dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable, Swatinem/rust-cache@v2, then a run step; codegen-drift invokes `cargo run -p aion-cli --bin aion -- generate ...`, so use `cargo run -p aion-cli --bin aion -- awl check <file>` the same way, or build the binary once and invoke it) running `aion awl check` over crates/aion-awl/tests/fixtures/*.awl that asserts the must-pass set {hello.awl, research_report.awl, research_report.canonical.awl} exits 0 and holds the two bounded_cycle fixtures {bounded_cycle.awl, bounded_cycle.canonical.awl} as an EXPLICIT expected failure that FAILS THE JOB if either ever exits 0, with an inline comment pointing at AWL1-004 (typed child contracts) as the brief that retires the carve-out (move the fixture into the must-pass set) at that point. Do NOT wire the aspirational example docs approval_gate.awl/sized_fanout.awl (they are AWL-1 documentation ahead of the implementation — AWL1-005 / AWL1-002 — out of scope). (2) crate-wide zero-bypass permanent gate — add a job/step (mirror `no-norn-in-platform`) running `! grep -rnE '#!?\[(allow|expect)|#\[ignore|include!\(' crates/aion-awl/` so any future bypass in src OR tests dies in CI forever. 001e-a made this grep GREEN; this brief wires it as the permanent guard (name only crates/aion-awl/, which exists — avoid the ugrep exit-2 false-green trap). Do NOT edit any file outside .github/workflows/ci.yml — no src, no test, no fixture/.golden/.canonical, no Cargo.toml, no other crate, no doc; do NOT edit a fixture to make the checker pass and do NOT re-open any bypass 001e-a purged. The full R1-R3 contract with acceptance criteria is committed at docs/design/aion-authoring/awl/briefs/AWL0-REFAC-001e-b-input.json — read it in the workspace.
brief.context:
Part B of the two-way split of the AWL0-REFAC-001e hygiene finale (operator ruling 2026-07-09: the finale was authored whole but is too heavy for one dispatch, so it is dispatched as 001e-a HYGIENE PURGE and 001e-b CI WIRING, with 001e-b depending on 001e-a landing; see BRIEF-CRAFT entry 10 / generalised principle 5). 001e-a purged the last bypasses (ast.rs 190 docs, printer.rs vestigial deletion, the three test files' 55 sites converted to Result-returning tests) and made crates/aion-awl/ zero-bypass a TRUE fact; 001e-b makes that state PERMANENT and closes the CI blind spot that hid the bounded_cycle checker failures. This brief is DEPENDENT on 001e-a: at dispatch crates/aion-awl/ carries ZERO bypasses (so the crate-wide grep is already GREEN — this brief wires it as a permanent GUARD, not to flip it) and the crate is fully split under the 500-line limit. Stamped from the 001c/001d/001e templates. 001a merged adb6e4cf; 001b merged 80426594; 001c salvaged+merged 9800b9c4; 001d preceded this; 001e-a is this brief's prerequisite. This brief owns EXACTLY ONE file — .github/workflows/ci.yml.

THIS BRIEF IS CI WIRING ONLY — no Rust changes, a tiny single-file diff. CI lives in .github/workflows/ci.yml (fast lane: changes / fmt / clippy / no-norn-in-platform / standalone-crates / test / codegen-drift; slow lane: kill9-failover). The additive-job model is established: `codegen-drift` builds/runs the aion binary (`cargo run -p aion-cli --bin aion -- generate ... --check`) and `no-norn-in-platform` runs a mechanical assertion (`cargo xtask no-norn-in-platform`). Mirror those.

INTENTION: The monolithic AWL0-REFAC-001 failed twice — once by fraud (include! splices + crate-wide allow), once by an honest rollback after cascading errors — so it was decomposed smallest-first, then the finale itself was split for right-sizing. Every non-negotiable here is a mechanical gate (three GREEN on base: scope-fence, no-bypasses-crate-wide, awl-check-local; one RED on base BY DESIGN: ci-wired, because it IS the work). The scope fence makes wandering impossible, and the measured pass/fail split is embedded so the developer neither wires phantom fixtures nor improvises the divergences.

CONSTRAINTS:
- CN1: Canonical docs are the reading list; the plan's Wave 0 AWL0-REFAC-001e entry (the CI-wiring half) is the CONTRACT — implement it verbatim. The gate is scoped to the CHECKER-FIXTURE set crates/aion-awl/tests/fixtures/*.awl (five files, of which exactly the two bounded_cycle fixtures fail), which makes the plan's `two bounded_cycle fixtures` carve-out exactly complete; the two aspirational example docs are out of scope (see DIVERGENCES). Discovered ambiguities are findings to report, never license to improvise.
- CN2: Coding standards (CLAUDE.md): no #[allow]/#[expect]/#[ignore] bypasses. This brief adds the CI gate that ENFORCES that standard crate-wide forever. It changes no Rust; the one discipline that matters is not re-opening any bypass 001e-a purged.
- CN3: Gate argv, run EXACTLY in order before reporting: mechanical gates (scope-fence, no-bypasses-crate-wide, ci-wired, awl-check-local) -> cargo fmt -> cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings -> cargo test -p aion-awl -> cargo test -p aion-cli.
- CN4: Round-trip property is sacred and the emitter goldens are byte-pinned; this brief touches only ci.yml so it cannot perturb either (the scope fence forbids touching any src/test/fixture/golden).
- CN5: Review lenses: the standard adversarial set PLUS a spec-fidelity lens — clause-by-clause against R1-R3, especially that the bounded_cycle carve-out is an EXPLICIT, mechanically-visible expected-failure pointing at AWL1-004 (a silent skip or a fixture edited/deleted to pass is BLOCKING) and the aspirational examples were not wired. All four lenses carry the docs-quality clause (a placeholder is a blocking finding — here the AWL1-004 carve-out comment must be a real, informative pointer, not a bare label) and the check-base-before-labeling-regression clause (BRIEF-CRAFT entry 16).
- CN6: Bar reminders: stage explicit paths (never `git add -A`); inspect with `git diff --no-ext-diff`; NEVER trust the workflow's own green — the operator re-runs the gates by hand before merge, including running the awl-check job's shell locally.

The full R1-R3 contract with acceptance criteria is committed at docs/design/aion-authoring/awl/briefs/AWL0-REFAC-001e-b-input.json — read it in the workspace.
pointers: docs/design/aion-authoring/awl/briefs/AWL0-REFAC-001e-a-input.json (the prerequisite purge that turned the crate-wide grep green)
docs/design/aion-authoring/awl/briefs/AWL0-REFAC-001e-input.json (the SUPERSEDED whole finale — retained for the measured CI-recon and divergences; split into 001e-a + 001e-b by operator ruling)

docs/design/aion-authoring/awl/AWL-EXECUTION-PLAN.md (Wave 0 — the AWL0-REFAC-001e entry: `wire aion awl check over every committed .awl fixture in CI ... EXCEPT the two bounded_cycle fixtures ... pointing at AWL1-004 ... the crate-wide zero-bypass gate goes green here and stays a permanent gate`)
docs/design/aion-authoring/awl/BRIEF-CRAFT.md (why the gates are shaped this way; principle 10 MEASURE-before-mandating, entry 16 check-base-before-regression, the ugrep exit-2 false-green trap)

.github/workflows/ci.yml (the fast-lane keystone CI; mirror the additive `codegen-drift` job — `cargo run -p aion-cli --bin aion -- generate ... --check` — and the mechanical `no-norn-in-platform` job)

crates/aion-cli/src/awl.rs (the `aion awl check` subcommand — `check <file>` parses+typechecks one .awl, exits non-zero + prints `<file>:<line>:<col>: error: <message>` on error, `ok: <file> (N steps)` on success)

crates/aion-awl/tests/fixtures/ (the five committed checker fixtures: hello.awl, research_report.awl, research_report.canonical.awl PASS; bounded_cycle.awl, bounded_cycle.canonical.awl FAIL = the carve-out)
docs/design/aion-authoring/awl/examples/approval_gate.awl and sized_fanout.awl (the aspirational AWL-1 example docs — DO NOT wire; out of scope)
scope_in: .github/workflows/ci.yml (add the awl-check fixtures job with the bounded_cycle expected-failures carve-out pointing at AWL1-004, and the permanent crate-wide zero-bypass grep gate)
scope_out: CI CARVE-OUT MUST BE MECHANICALLY VISIBLE (R1): the two bounded_cycle fixtures are an EXPECTED failure that fails the job if either ever checks clean, with an inline AWL1-004 pointer. A silent skip, a deleted fixture, or a fixture edited to pass are each BLOCKING. Do NOT wire approval_gate.awl/sized_fanout.awl (aspirational AWL-1 docs) into this gate.

PERMANENT CRATE-WIDE GATE, NAMED PATHS ONLY (R2): wire `! grep ... crates/aion-awl/` over the path that EXISTS (no ugrep exit-2 false-green). It is GREEN at this brief's base (001e-a purged the last five bypasses) — a permanent guard, not a red-by-design flip.

SCOPE FENCE (R3): the diff touches ONLY .github/workflows/ci.yml. Every src/test/fixture/golden/canonical file, crates/aion-awl/Cargo.toml, and every other crate are UNMODIFIED. Do NOT edit a fixture to make the checker pass; do NOT re-open a purged bypass. If you believe an edit outside ci.yml is required, STOP and report it as a finding.
ZERO behavior change to the crate: CI gates only — no src/test/fixture/golden/manifest change; the round-trip property and emitter goldens are untouched.
NO LINT-POLICY WEAKENING: crates/aion-awl/Cargo.toml byte-identical; no bypass re-introduced anywhere (001e-a's zero-bypass state is preserved and now guarded).
SHALL NOT touch any crate, any src or test file, or any doc — ONLY .github/workflows/ci.yml.
acceptance: scope-fence gate (GREEN on base): git diff versus the pinned base SHA, excluding only .github/workflows/ci.yml, is empty.
no-bypasses-crate-wide gate (GREEN invariant on base — 001e-a landed): `! grep -rnE '#!?\[(allow|expect)|#\[ignore|include!\(' crates/aion-awl/` exits 0; this brief must not re-open any bypass.

ci-wired gate (RED on base BY DESIGN): ci.yml wires the `aion awl check` fixtures job, the AWL1-004 carve-out pointer, and the crate-wide bypass-pattern permanent gate — red on base, green when all three are wired.

awl-check-local gate (behavioural guard, GREEN on base): builds aion, runs check over tests/fixtures/*.awl (the SAME shell the CI job runs), asserts must-pass set exits 0 and the two bounded_cycle fixtures exit non-zero (fails if a bounded_cycle fixture is edited to pass — guards against silent green).
cargo fmt -> cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings -> cargo test -p aion-awl -> cargo test -p aion-cli, all clean (green invariants — no Rust changes).

Hand review: ci.yml's awl-check job has the mechanically-visible bounded_cycle expected-failure carve-out pointing at AWL1-004 (not a silent skip), the aspirational examples excluded; the crate-wide zero-bypass grep is wired permanently over crates/aion-awl/ (named path exists); no fixture/golden/canonical/src/test/Cargo.toml edit; the crate-wide no-bypasses grep is GREEN.
brief.notes:
DISPATCH HISTORY (read this first): the parent brief AWL0-REFAC-001 was dispatched twice and REJECTED twice at operator review, then decomposed into five one-concern sub-briefs. Dispatch 1 faked the split with include!("parts/part_00.rs") textual splices behind 2-line mod.rs files plus a crate-wide #![allow(...)]. Dispatch 2 rolled the split back after cascading errors and instead replaced aion-awl's Cargo.toml `[lints] workspace = true` with a private allow-list to silence clippy. BOTH escape hatches are now mechanical gates; this brief WIRES one of them (the crate-wide zero-bypass grep) into CI so it stays enforced forever. The pilots landed FIRST-PASS clean: 001a (checker split) merged adb6e4cf, 001b (lib.rs split + 4-attr purge) merged 80426594; 001c (parser split + allow-block purge) ended cycle_cap_exhausted, was salvaged on-branch, and merged 9800b9c4; 001d (emitter pure split) preceded this; 001e-a (this brief's prerequisite purge) makes the crate zero-bypass. Honest reporting was noted favourably every time: if something genuinely cannot be done within the boundaries, say so plainly in your report rather than working around a gate.

THIS IS THE HYGIENE FINALE, PART B (the CI WIRING). It is a TINY diff scoped to ONE file (.github/workflows/ci.yml). The purge half (ast.rs docs + printer.rs vestigial deletion + the three test files) is 001e-a, which lands BEFORE this dispatch. Do NOT touch any Rust, any fixture, or any manifest — only ci.yml.

TECHNIQUE: add an additive fast-lane job to .github/workflows/ci.yml. Mirror `codegen-drift` (checkout + dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable + Swatinem/rust-cache@v2 + a run step that builds/runs the aion binary — codegen-drift uses `cargo run -p aion-cli --bin aion -- generate examples/order-saga --check`) and `no-norn-in-platform` (a mechanical assertion job). The awl-check job runs `aion awl check` over each crates/aion-awl/tests/fixtures/*.awl (via `cargo run -p aion-cli --bin aion -- awl check <file>`, or build once then invoke `target/debug/aion awl check <file>`), asserts {hello.awl, research_report.awl, research_report.canonical.awl} exit 0, and holds {bounded_cycle.awl, bounded_cycle.canonical.awl} as an EXPECTED failure that FAILS THE JOB if either exits 0, with an inline comment naming AWL1-004 and instructing the reader to retire the carve-out (move the fixture into the must-pass set) when AWL1-004 lands. Also add the permanent crate-wide zero-bypass grep gate (`! grep -rnE '#!?\[(allow|expect)|#\[ignore|include!\(' crates/aion-awl/`) — name only crates/aion-awl/, which exists. Do NOT wire approval_gate.awl/sized_fanout.awl. A robust shell shape for the awl-check job: build the binary once (`cargo build -p aion-cli --bin aion`), set `B=target/debug/aion`, loop the must-pass set failing on any non-zero, loop the bounded_cycle set failing on any ZERO (with the AWL1-004 message), `exit $s`. This is exactly the awl-check-local gate below — the developer proves the CI logic locally before pushing.

MEASURED CI RECON (authoring, RE-RUN on base fab18d2373dd22b2f2794ede8ce02c41d33234d6 = current main after 001c's salvage landing). `aion awl check <file>` (crates/aion-cli/src/awl.rs) parses+typechecks ONE .awl, prints `ok: <file> (N steps)` + exit 0 on success, a `<file>:<line>:<col>: error: <message>` diagnostic + non-zero exit on failure. Authoring ran it over the five committed checker fixtures: crates/aion-awl/tests/fixtures/hello.awl -> `ok: ... (2 steps)` exit 0; research_report.awl -> `ok: ... (7 steps)` exit 0; research_report.canonical.awl -> `ok: ... (7 steps)` exit 0; bounded_cycle.awl -> exit 1 `crates/aion-awl/tests/fixtures/bounded_cycle.awl:22:3: error: as binding \`state\` expected ReviewState, found untyped child result`; bounded_cycle.canonical.awl -> exit 1 `...bounded_cycle.canonical.awl:23:3: error: as binding \`state\` expected ReviewState, found untyped child result`. So the must-pass set is {hello, research_report, research_report.canonical} and the expected-fail carve-out is exactly {bounded_cycle, bounded_cycle.canonical}. The whole-crate bypass grep on this base returns ONLY the five files 001e-a purges — after 001e-a lands the grep is GREEN, so the permanent crate-wide gate this brief wires is a green invariant at dispatch. CI lives in .github/workflows/ci.yml; the additive-job convention is `codegen-drift` (build/run the aion binary) and `no-norn-in-platform` (mechanical assertion).

DIVERGENCES (plan vs measured reality — resolved at authoring, stated as findings per CN1): the plan says wire `aion awl check` over `every committed .awl fixture ... EXCEPT the two bounded_cycle fixtures`. MEASURED: `aion awl check` over EVERY committed .awl in the repo yields FOUR failures — the two bounded_cycle fixtures AND the two aspirational AWL-1 EXAMPLE docs (docs/design/aion-authoring/awl/examples/approval_gate.awl = `unknown step field \`otherwise\``/AWL1-005, sized_fanout.awl = `type declaration needs record fields`/AWL1-002). Resolved: the gate is scoped to the CHECKER FIXTURE set crates/aion-awl/tests/fixtures/*.awl (five files, of which exactly the two bounded_cycle fixtures fail), which makes the plan's `two bounded_cycle fixtures` carve-out EXACTLY complete and matches the gate's stated purpose (`closes the blind spot that hid the bounded_cycle checker failures`). The two aspirational example docs live under docs/design/.../examples/ (labeled `AWL-1, aspirational` in the plan), are documentation ahead of the implementation, and are OUT OF this checker-fixtures gate's scope — not carve-outs. A future AWL-1 brief may add an examples-check gate; that is out of 001e-b's contract.

SIZING: this is the LIGHTER half of the split finale — a single-file ci.yml diff (two additive gates) with a measured, deterministic pass/fail split. It fits the one-dev-round + 3-fix-cycle envelope comfortably. The only skill required is careful GitHub-Actions YAML plus a robust bash loop for the must-pass / expected-fail split; the awl-check-local gate proves that loop before push.

GATES: THREE are GREEN ON BASE (any red in them is yours): scope-fence (exit 0 on the clean tree; correctly exit 1 on any non-ci.yml edit; exit 0 on a synthetic ci.yml-only edit), no-bypasses-crate-wide (exit 0 — 001e-a landed, crates/aion-awl/ carries zero bypasses; this brief must keep it green), awl-check-local (exit 0 — must-pass fixtures pass, bounded_cycle fixtures fail-as-expected; it runs the SAME shell the CI job will run). ONE is RED ON BASE BY DESIGN because it IS the work: ci-wired (exit 1 on base — ci.yml has none of the `awl check` / `AWL1-004` / crate-wide-grep wiring yet; green when all three are wired — the gate greps ci.yml for `awl check`, `AWL1-004`, and `include!` [the last is the literal string inside the crate-wide grep pattern, present once the permanent gate is wired]). The scope-fence gate is pinned to a base SHA: the literal string PINNED_SHA_AT_DISPATCH in the scope-fence argv MUST be replaced by the operator with `git rev-parse main` at dispatch time — 001e-a (and possibly other work) will have merged since authoring, so main WILL have moved and the pin MUST be refreshed; the operator re-verifies the three green-on-base gates green + the one red-on-base gate red on that fresh base before dispatching. This brief's dispatch REQUIRES 001e-a landed first (depends_on) so the crate-wide grep is green. The test gates are scoped (aion-awl + aion-cli) and deterministic — the flaky workspace suite is deliberately NOT a gate here (tasks #243/#244: aion-package `append_run_regenerates_type_checking_gleam` and aion-server --lib both flake under load in crates this diff never touches — and this diff touches only ci.yml); if a workspace test outside aion-awl/aion-cli ever runs and flakes while every in-scope gate passes and your diff touches only ci.yml, re-run once and report the flake honestly rather than attempting an out-of-scope fix. The operator runs the full workspace suite by hand at merge review, and additionally re-runs the awl-check job's shell locally to confirm the CI logic before merge.

gates.name: awl-check-local
argv: cargo build -p aion-cli --bin aion -q && B=target/debug/aion; s=0; for f in crates/aion-awl/tests/fixtures/hello.awl crates/aion-awl/tests/fixtures/research_report.awl crates/aion-awl/tests/fixtures/research_report.canonical.awl; do "$B" awl check "$f" >/dev/null 2>&1 || { echo "MUST-PASS FAILED: $f"; s=1; }; done; for f in crates/aion-awl/tests/fixtures/bounded_cycle.awl crates/aion-awl/tests/fixtures/bounded_cycle.canonical.awl; do "$B" awl check "$f" >/dev/null 2>&1 && { echo "EXPECTED-FAIL NOW GREEN (AWL1-004 landed - retire carve-out): $f"; s=1; }; done; exit $s

lenses.name: correctness
lenses.charter: Hunt defects in the change itself: a CI job that does not actually enforce what it claims. The highest-value defect classes here: (1) an awl-check job whose bash does not actually FAIL when a bounded_cycle fixture starts passing (the guard-against-silent-green must be real — an `|| true`, a missing `exit $s`, a subshell that swallows the exit code, or a `continue-on-error: true` on the job defeats it); (2) a must-pass loop that passes even when a fixture regresses (e.g. exit code not checked, or `set -e` interaction hiding it); (3) the crate-wide grep gate naming a path that might not exist and false-greening via the ugrep exit-2 trap (crates/aion-awl/ exists, so name exactly that). Construct the state that proves the gate is hollow — e.g. imagine AWL1-004 landing and bounded_cycle passing: does the job go red? Imagine a bypass added to lexer/: does the crate-wide gate go red? Before labeling a defect a REGRESSION, read the base ci.yml (`git show <base>:.github/workflows/ci.yml`) — a faithfully preserved pre-existing CI quirk is a finding-for-follow-up, not a blocking rejection (BRIEF-CRAFT entry 16).

lenses.name: regressions
lenses.charter: Hunt collateral damage: behaviour the diff changes that the brief did not ask to change. This brief touches ONLY .github/workflows/ci.yml — any edit to a src/test/fixture/golden/canonical file or crates/aion-awl/Cargo.toml is an automatic regression (and a scope-fence failure). Watch specifically for a fixture edited or deleted to make the checker pass (BLOCKING) and for any bypass 001e-a purged being re-opened (the crate-wide grep must stay green). Also watch that the new awl-check job does not break or duplicate an existing job (name collision, `needs:` cycle, or a matrix that reruns the whole suite). The existing fast-lane jobs (fmt/clippy/test/codegen-drift/no-norn-in-platform) must be unchanged except for the two additive gates. Before labeling a preserved pre-existing CI behaviour a regression, read the base ci.yml first (BRIEF-CRAFT entry 16).

lenses.name: brief_compliance
lenses.charter: Hold the diff to the brief, adversarially: every acceptance criterion either demonstrably met or a blocking finding; every scope_out boundary respected; every claimed deviation actually declared. Pass-and-still-wrong is the failure class — do not accept a claim without evidence in the diff or gate output. Confirm specifically: R1 the awl-check job runs over crates/aion-awl/tests/fixtures/*.awl, asserts the must-pass set exits 0, and holds the two bounded_cycle fixtures as an expected failure that FAILS THE JOB if either checks clean (not a silent skip), with a real inline AWL1-004 pointer comment (a bare label is a docs-quality finding), and the aspirational examples approval_gate.awl/sized_fanout.awl are NOT wired; R2 the crate-wide zero-bypass grep is wired permanently over crates/aion-awl/ (named path exists) and Cargo.toml is byte-identical; R3 only .github/workflows/ci.yml changed. Run the awl-check job's shell yourself to confirm it is not hollow.

lenses.name: spec_fidelity
lenses.charter: Read AWL-EXECUTION-PLAN.md Wave 0 (the AWL0-REFAC-001e entry — `wire aion awl check over every committed .awl fixture in CI ... EXCEPT the two bounded_cycle fixtures ... pointing at AWL1-004 ... the crate-wide zero-bypass gate goes green here and stays a permanent gate`) and the committed brief docs/design/aion-authoring/awl/briefs/AWL0-REFAC-001e-b-input.json, and check the implementation clause by clause against R1-R3. This brief is finale PART B (the CI WIRING); the purge half (ast.rs docs + printer.rs + the three test files) is 001e-a, which landed first — so any Rust/fixture/manifest edit here is a finding. (R1) `aion awl check` is wired over crates/aion-awl/tests/fixtures/*.awl with the two bounded_cycle fixtures held as an EXPLICIT, mechanically-visible expected-failure that FAILS THE JOB if either checks clean, pointing at AWL1-004 — a silent skip, a deleted fixture, or a fixture edited to pass are BLOCKING; the aspirational examples approval_gate.awl/sized_fanout.awl are correctly EXCLUDED (they are AWL-1 documentation ahead of the implementation — verify they were not wired and not edited). (R2) the crate-wide zero-bypass grep is wired as a PERMANENT CI gate over crates/aion-awl/ (named path exists, no ugrep exit-2 false-green); crates/aion-awl/Cargo.toml [lints] is byte-identical (workspace = true only). (R3) the diff touches ONLY .github/workflows/ci.yml. ANY fixture/golden edit, any src/test/Cargo.toml edit, any silent carve-out, any re-opened bypass, or any change outside ci.yml is an automatic blocking finding — both prior dispatches of the parent brief were rejected for the fraud patterns. Before calling a preserved pre-existing CI behaviour a regression, read the base ci.yml first (BRIEF-CRAFT entry 16).
